condition_specific: smoke inhalation of the lungs or respiratory tract resulting in total disability or death
notes: Covers uniformed members of PAID fire departments only. City or town may adopt this presumption by ordinance — not automatically statewide. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence. Pre-employment (or subsequent) physical must not have revealed evidence of condition.

Rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence. For firefighters hired AFTER July 1, 2023: presumption does not apply if (1) pre-employment physical revealed heart disease or hypertension, or (2) firefighter regularly/habitually used tobacco products during the 5 years prior to diagnosis. No such restrictions for firefighters hired before July 1, 2023.

CONCLUSIVE (irrebuttable) presumption under § 45-19.1-4 — any type of cancer in a firefighter is conclusively presumed occupational. For firefighters hired AFTER July 22, 2020: presumption does not apply if (1) pre-employment physical revealed cancer, (2) fewer than 2 years of employment, or (3) regular/habitual tobacco use in 5 years prior to diagnosis. Applies retroactively to cancer claims pending on or after July 22, 2020. Covers active firefighters (during service) and retired municipal firefighters (§ 45-19.1-3 — covers retired members retroactively).

PTSD must be diagnosed by licensed mental health professional with a master's degree. Must arise from exposure to potentially traumatic events in course of employment or off-duty emergency assistance within Rhode Island. Presumption is rebuttable by fair preponderance of evidence that PTSI/PTSD is not job-related. PTSD arising from disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination or adverse job action not covered.

Explicitly covers police officers (including chiefs, municipal officers, deputy sheriffs, fugitive task force, capitol police, environmental police criminal investigators, airport police). Same terms as firefighters. PTSD from adverse job actions not covered.

condition_specific: infectious disease including HIV (HBV, HCV) and other transmissible diseases
notes: Infectious disease defined as any condition transmissible by association with the sick or their secretions/excretions, excluding common cold; explicitly includes HIV, HBV, HCV. Disease must develop or manifest as a result of exposure during period while fire fighter or police officer is in service of department. Covers paid and volunteer fire departments. Entitles to occupational disability benefits under ch. 19 of title 45.

condition_specific: infectious disease including HIV (HBV, HCV) and other transmissible diseases
notes: Statute explicitly covers both fire fighters AND police officers. Disease must manifest during period of active service. Same definition of infectious disease as firefighter row.
